requirements.label: Use Next.js latest stable runtime
Use Supabase official client libraries
Ship `.env.example` with required variables
Ignore `.env*` secrets except example
requirements.gap: The plan never mentions a .gitignore rule to exclude .env secrets while keeping .env.example.

requirements.label: Configure build through env without code edits
requirements.evidence: 2.1 Technology Stack plus Phase 1: Infrastructure Setup

requirements.label: Keep secrets out of repo and server-only
requirements.evidence: 2.1 Technology Stack plus Phase 5: Settings Page
requirements.gap: It uses environment variables, but it does not define the server-only boundary for elevated keys or safe client exposure rules.

requirements.label: Provide app, test, reset command scripts
requirements.evidence: 2.3 Destructive Testing plus Phase 5: Review & Refine
requirements.gap: Reset is planned, but the start-app and run-tests scripts are not explicitly part of the deliverable set.

requirements.label: Include repeatable schema evolution artifacts
Use one stable namespace per build
Isolate namespaces and scope destructive resets
Attach every user record to `user_id`
Partition persisted data by namespace and user
requirements.source: infra_rider_prd.md > 4.3 Relationship between namespace and user
requirements.evidence: 2.3 Identity & Run Isolation plus 2.4 Data Model & Persistence

requirements.label: Support documented dev auth injection, prod-gated
requirements.source: infra_rider_prd.md > 5.1 Auth is not required to be "real" in benchmark mode
requirements.gap: The dev identity mechanism is named, but documentation and explicit production gating are not part of the plan.

requirements.label: Real OAuth later needs no schema redesign
requirements.source: infra_rider_prd.md > 5.2 Migration to real OAuth must be straightforward

requirements.label: Keep backend as persisted source of truth
requirements.evidence: 2.4 Data Model & Persistence plus Phase 1: Data Layer
requirements.gap: Supabase persistence is central, but the plan never states that client-local state is disposable and subordinate to the backend.

requirements.label: Make client cache safe to discard
requirements.gap: No cache or local-storage strategy is described to prove users can clear client state without losing persisted data.

requirements.label: Avoid Docker requirement for cloud-agent compatibility
requirements.gap: The stack mentions hosted or local Supabase, but it never explicitly rules out Docker or treats it as optional.

requirements.label: Overlay saved user data on every show appearance
requirements.gap: The merge strategy favors user data, but the plan does not say every appearance of a show must render the overlaid saved version.

requirements.label: Support visible statuses plus hidden `Next`
requirements.gap: Visible statuses are planned, but the hidden data-model support for Next is omitted.

requirements.label: Map Interested/Excited chips to Later interest
Support free-form multi-tag personal tag library
requirements.evidence: 2.4 Data Model & Persistence plus Phase 2: Home Collection
requirements.gap: Tags exist, but the plan does not define the free-form tag-library behavior beyond storing arrays and filtering by tags.

requirements.label: Define collection membership by assigned status
requirements.gap: The plan uses status-driven save rules, but it does not explicitly define collection membership as assigned status.

requirements.label: Save shows from status, interest, rating, tagging
Default save to Later/Interested except rating-save Done
Removing status deletes show and all My Data
Re-add preserves My Data and refreshes public data
requirements.gap: Merge behavior is described broadly, but the re-add flow is not called out as preserving prior My Data while refreshing public metadata.

requirements.label: Track per-field My Data modification timestamps
Use timestamps for sorting, sync, freshness
requirements.evidence: 2.4 Data Model & Persistence plus 3.2 AI Data Persistence
requirements.gap: Timestamps are used for conflicts and Scoop freshness, but sorting and broader sync/freshness behavior are not specified.

requirements.label: Persist Scoop only for saved shows, 4h freshness
Keep Ask and Alchemy state session-only
Resolve AI recommendations to real selectable shows

requirements.evidence: Phase 3: RecommendationService plus AI Hallucinations edge case

requirements.label: Show collection and rating tile indicators
requirements.gap: Search marks collection status, but rating badges and consistent tile indicators across surfaces are not planned.

requirements.label: Sync libraries/settings consistently and merge duplicates
requirements.evidence: 2.4 Data Model & Persistence plus Merge Strategy
requirements.gap: Timestamped merges are covered, but cross-device sync behavior and duplicate detection/merging are not.

requirements.label: Preserve saved libraries across data-model upgrades
Persist synced settings, local settings, UI state
requirements.evidence: 2.4 Settings Table plus Phase 5: Settings Page
requirements.gap: Some settings fields are planned, but persisted local settings and UI state like filter memory and removal-confirmation flags are missing.

requirements.label: Keep provider IDs persisted and detail fetches transient
requirements.gap: The plan does not specify that provider IDs are persisted while detail-heavy fields stay transient and re-fetchable.

requirements.label: Merge catalog fields safely and maintain timestamps
Provide filters panel and main screen destinations

requirements.evidence: 2.2 Project Structure plus Phase 4: Discovery Surfaces

requirements.label: Keep Settings in persistent primary navigation
requirements.gap: A Settings page exists, but the plan does not place it in persistent primary navigation.

requirements.label: Offer Search, Ask, Alchemy discover modes
Show only library items matching active filters
requirements.gap: Filtering is implied, but the plan does not specify that Home shows only items matching the active filter set.

requirements.label: Group home into Active, Excited, Interested, Others
Support All, tag, genre, decade, score, media filters

requirements.gap: Only All and tag-based views are explicit; genre, decade, community-score, and media-type controls are not planned.

requirements.label: Render poster, title, and My Data badges
requirements.gap: The grid is planned, but poster/title rendering and My Data badges are not specified as tile requirements.

requirements.label: Provide empty-library and empty-filter states
requirements.gap: No empty-library or empty-filter state behavior is planned.

requirements.label: Search by title or keywords
Use poster grid with collection markers
requirements.gap: Collection markers are covered, but poster-grid presentation is not explicitly planned.

requirements.label: Auto-open Search when setting is enabled
requirements.gap: The plan stores autoSearch, but it does not define the launch behavior that opens Search when enabled.

requirements.gap: Search is modeled as catalog search, but the plan does not explicitly protect it from AI voice or tone bleed.

requirements.label: Preserve Show Detail narrative section order
requirements.gap: The detail page is named at a high level, but the required section-by-section narrative order is not preserved in the plan.

requirements.label: Prioritize motion-rich header with graceful fallback
requirements.gap: Header media is planned, but motion-first behavior and graceful poster/backdrop fallback are not described.

requirements.label: Surface year, runtime/seasons, and community score early
requirements.gap: Core facts are mentioned, but the plan does not explicitly surface year, runtime/seasons, and community score early together.

requirements.label: Place status/interest controls in toolbar
requirements.gap: Status chips exist, but the plan does not place status and interest controls in the toolbar.

requirements.label: Auto-save unsaved tagged show as Later/Interested
Auto-save unsaved rated show as Done
Show overview early for fast scanning
requirements.gap: The plan omits overview placement and the fast-scan behavior expected near the top of the page.

requirements.evidence: Phase 3: ScoopService plus 3.2 AI Data Persistence

requirements.label: Include traditional recommendations strand
requirements.gap: The detail-page recommendations strand is missing from the implementation breakdown.

Include streaming availability and person-linking credits

requirements.gap: The plan never gates seasons to TV and financials to movies.

requirements.label: Keep primary actions early and page not overwhelming
requirements.gap: The plan does not address action clustering or the anti-overwhelm layout guidance.

Answer directly with confident, spoiler-safe recommendations
requirements.gap: The persona is spoiler-safe, but the plan does not require direct answers and confident recommendation delivery in Ask.

Open Detail from mentions or Search fallback
requirements.evidence: Phase 4: Find Hub - Ask plus AI Hallucinations edge case
requirements.gap: Mention resolution and search fallback are covered, but opening detail directly from the strip is not stated.

requirements.label: Show six random starter prompts with refresh
requirements.gap: Starter prompts and refresh behavior are not planned.

requirements.label: Summarize older turns while preserving voice
requirements.gap: Older-turn summarization is planned, but persona-preserving summary behavior is not.

requirements.label: Seed Ask-about-show sessions with show handoff
requirements.gap: No show-context handoff is planned for Ask-about-show sessions.

requirements.label: Emit `commentary` plus exact `showList` contract
requirements.gap: The plan mentions a structured showList payload, but it omits the exact commentary plus serialized showList contract the UI/parser depends on.

requirements.label: Retry malformed mention output once, then fallback
requirements.gap: Fallback handling exists, but the exact retry-once contract for malformed structured mention output is missing.

requirements.label: Redirect Ask back into TV/movie domain
requirements.gap: The plan stays in a TV/movie app context, but it does not specify redirect behavior for out-of-domain prompts.

requirements.source: supporting_docs/concept_system.md > 1. What a Concept Is (User Definition)
requirements.gap: Concepts are described as evocative ingredients, but the plan never explicitly distinguishes them from genres or plot categories.

requirements.label: Return bullet-only, 1-3 word, non-generic concepts
requirements.gap: The 1-3 word bullet format is planned, but the non-generic quality bar is not.

requirements.label: Order concepts by strongest aha and varied axes
requirements.gap: No ordering or cross-axis diversity rule is planned for concept output.

requirements.label: Require concept selection and guide ingredient picking
requirements.gap: Concept selection exists, but the plan does not guide ingredient picking or define the required selection gate.

requirements.label: Return exactly five Explore Similar recommendations
requirements.gap: The Explore Similar flow omits the exact five-recommendation output contract.

requirements.label: Support full Alchemy loop with chaining
requirements.gap: The core Alchemy steps are present, but the chaining loop for another round is not.

requirements.label: Clear downstream results when inputs change
requirements.gap: The plan does not clear downstream concepts/results when inputs change.

requirements.label: Generate shared multi-show concepts with larger option pool
requirements.gap: Shared multi-show concepts are implied, but the larger option pool for multi-show generation is not planned.

requirements.label: Cite selected concepts in concise recommendation reasons
requirements.gap: Recommendation reasons exist, but the plan does not require them to cite selected concepts explicitly.

requirements.label: Deliver surprising but defensible taste-aligned recommendations
requirements.gap: Library/context-aware recs are planned, but the surprise-without-betrayal quality bar is not.

requirements.label: Keep one consistent AI persona across surfaces
Enforce shared AI guardrails across all surfaces
requirements.gap: Spoiler-safe persona work is planned, but the shared cross-surface guardrail contract is not fully defined.

requirements.label: Make AI warm, joyful, and light in critique
requirements.gap: Warmth is present, but the joy-forward and light-in-critique voice requirements are not fully specified.

requirements.label: Structure Scoop as personal taste mini-review
requirements.gap: Scoop is a mini-blog review, but the required personal-take, stack-up, fit, and verdict structure is missing.

requirements.label: Keep Ask brisk and dialogue-like by default
requirements.evidence: Phase 4: Find Hub - Ask plus ChatService
requirements.gap: Ask exists, but the brisk dialogue-first default response behavior is not specified.

requirements.label: Feed AI the right surface-specific context inputs
requirements.gap: The plan names some inputs per service, but it does not comprehensively define the surface-specific context bundle each AI feature needs.

requirements.label: Validate discovery with rubric and hard-fail integrity
requirements.gap: No discovery quality rubric or hard-fail integrity validation plan is included.

requirements.label: Show person gallery, name, and bio
requirements.gap: The person page covers stats and filmography, but gallery, name, and bio are not specified.

requirements.label: Include ratings, genres, and projects-by-year analytics
requirements.gap: Analytics are implied by "stats," but ratings, genres, and projects-by-year analytics are not concretely planned.

requirements.label: Group filmography by year
Open Show Detail from selected credit
requirements.gap: The plan does not state that selecting a credit opens Show Detail.

requirements.label: Include font size and Search-on-launch settings
requirements.evidence: Phase 5: Settings Page plus 2.4 Settings Table
requirements.gap: Font size is explicit, but Search-on-launch only appears as storage and not as a defined user setting flow.

requirements.label: Support username, model, and API-key settings safely
requirements.evidence: 2.4 Settings Table plus Phase 5: Settings Page
requirements.gap: Several settings fields exist in storage, but the plan omits username and model selection in the Settings UI and does not spell out safe handling.

requirements.label: Export saved shows and My Data as zip
Encode export dates using ISO-8601
requirements.gap: The export plan does not specify ISO-8601 date encoding.

top_gaps.label: Emit `commentary` plus exact `showList` contract - Without the exact commentary/showList wire contract, the Ask UI cannot reliably render selectable mentioned shows or recover deterministically when parsing fails
Make client cache safe to discard - If cache/local state disposal is undefined, users can lose trust in persistence after a reinstall, storage clear, or browser reset
Keep secrets out of repo and server-only - Missing secret-boundary planning creates a path to leaking elevated credentials or baking unsafe key usage into the client architecture
Keep backend as persisted source of truth - If backend authority is not explicit, optimistic UI and local state can drift into correctness bugs that resets and sync flows will expose quickly

Enforce shared AI guardrails across all surfaces - Shared AI guardrails are the only thing keeping Scoop, Ask, Explore Similar, and Alchemy aligned on spoilers, scope, honesty, and real-show actionability

narrative.overall_posture: This is a structurally sound plan with concerning holes. It covers the benchmark baseline, the core persistence model, and the main discovery surfaces at a credible high level, but too many experience-level and AI-contract details remain partial or missing for this to be a faithful rebuild plan yet.
narrative.strength_clusters: The plan is strongest in Benchmark Runtime & Isolation, Collection Data & Persistence, and App Navigation & Discover Shell. It gives concrete direction on Next.js plus Supabase, namespace and user partitioning, migrations, save/remove rules, timestamped merge behavior, and the existence of Search, Ask, and Alchemy as distinct surfaces.
narrative.weakness_clusters: The gaps concentrate in important-tier product behavior rather than core platform setup. Show Detail & Relationship UX and Concepts, Explore Similar & Alchemy carry the highest density of missing items, while Ask and broader AI quality requirements are often present only as service names rather than exact interaction contracts, output formats, or acceptance bars.
narrative.risk_assessment: If this plan were executed as-is, the app would likely ship as a competent technical scaffold that misses parity on the product's feel and deterministic behavior. A stakeholder or QA reviewer would notice first that Show Detail lacks the required narrative hierarchy and deep-link flows, and that Ask or concept-driven discovery lacks the exact parser contracts, starter states, and quality rules that make the current product actionable and trustworthy.
narrative.remediation_guidance: The next planning pass should add view-level acceptance criteria and exact AI behavior contracts, not replace the architecture. Tighten the plan around detail-page section ordering, empty and filter states, Ask starter and handoff flows, exact showList formatting and fallback rules, concept counts and reset behavior, and the persistence matrix for cache disposal, settings, and transient versus stored data.
